title: Veggie Pizza
description: Crisp homemade crust, tangy tomato sauce, and stretchy cashew mozzarella—the pizza night craving without dairy cheese.
explanation: What people miss in pizza is usually melt and salt, not cow’s milk itself. A simple cashew mozzarella (or a good store-bought plant mozzarella) plus a hot oven gives stretch and browning that satisfy the craving.
This is a full meal-style pizza: vegetable-forward toppings, a yeasted base you can start the same evening, and clear swaps if you need gluten-free or nut-free.
ingredients: 2.25 cups All-purpose Flour or 50-50 atta + maida
1 cup Tomato Pizza Sauce (or cooked tomato-garlic-oregano puree)
Fresh basil or coriander to finish
Cashew mozzarella: 1/2 cup Cashews soaked 20 mins, 1/2 cup Water, 1 tbsp Tapioca starch or cornflour, 1 tbsp Nutritional Yeast, 1/2 tsp Salt, 1/2 tsp Lemon juice, 1/4 tsp Garlic powder
instructions: Dough: mix flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Add oil and warm water to form a soft dough. Knead 6–8 minutes until smooth. Oil the bowl, cover, and rise 45–60 minutes until doubled.

Cashew mozzarella: blend soaked cashews, water, tapioca, nutritional yeast, salt, lemon, and garlic powder until silky. Cook in a nonstick pan on medium, stirring, 3–5 minutes until thick, stretchy, and glossy. Cool slightly.
Heat oven to the highest safe setting (230–250°C). Place a tray or pizza stone inside to preheat.
Punch dough, roll or stretch into 1–2 thin bases on parchment. Spread sauce, leaving a small rim.
Scatter vegetables. Dollop or spread warm cashew mozzarella. Sprinkle oregano and chilli flakes.
Bake 10–14 minutes until crust is golden and cheese spots brown. Finish with basil. Rest 2 minutes, slice, and serve hot.
stepNotes: Thin crust needs a screaming-hot surface—preheat the tray so the base crisps.
Tapioca starch is what makes the cashew cheese stretch; without it you get a tasty cream, not melt.
Pat wet vegetables dry so the pizza does not steam.
If using store-bought mozzarella, check labels for casein or milk powder—many Indian processed cheese blends still contain dairy.
Dough can be made ahead and refrigerated after the first rise; bring to room temp before shaping.

variationSections.title: Cheese options
items: Store-bought plant mozzarella shreds when available.
White bean + cashew blend for a lower-fat stretchy sauce.
Nut-free: potato-carrot “cheese” sauce with nutritional yeast and a little oil (different texture, still satisfying).

variationSections.title: Crust and diet
items: Gluten-free: use a trusted GF pizza mix or cauliflower base—follow pack liquid ratios.
Whole-wheat: 100% atta works but needs slightly more water and a bit more oil for tenderness.
No-onion/no-garlic: skip onion and garlic powder; load peppers, tomato, olives, oregano, and chilli.

variationSections.title: Topping themes
items: BBQ tofu crumble and red onion.
Jalapeño-corn for a desi-cafe style pie.
Margherita: sauce, cashew mozzarella, tomato, basil only.
